title: Dispatch-tasks pipeline: capture deferred items and route via typed runners and destinations

content.title: Dispatch-tasks pipeline: capture deferred items and route via typed runners and destinations
content.summary: A grill session on the seed->task pipeline, resumed to resolve the two open scoping threads. Diagnosis (unchanged): to-tasks silently drops seed deferred[] items. Decision: capture deferred items into the task file and build a general execution layer. to-tasks emits one cohesive task file of mixed items, each tagged with task_type/domain/deliverable. A new re-entrant dispatch-tasks routes items via a two-mode runners map (inline-skill vs spawn-agent), running one task_type branch per invocation with the JSON as the durable state machine. run-tasks is renamed build-code (inline code runner); a new export-tasks (spawn-agent, absorbing tasks-to-linear) ships captured items to destinations chosen by (deliverable, domain). This iteration resolved both open threads: (1) config lives centralized — the two global routing maps in one manifest resources/task-routing.json (keys runners + destinations, modeled on model-tiers.json), and the per-repo home domain as a new domain field on existing resources/repo-policy.json read via gx_load_policy; (2) v1 ships two destination adapters, GitHub Issues (code) + personal Notion (non-code), with Linear deferred. Vocabulary simplified to plain repo words: runners, destinations, wall. One correction folded in: Spawned Sapien's destination is its own Linear workspace, not a Notion DB. All threads resolved, so status is ready.
decisions: The deferred[] drop at the to-tasks boundary is a defect: to-tasks must capture deferred items into the task file rather than silently discarding them. out_of_scope[] (negative filter) and disposed_threads[] (settled/rejected) remain correctly handled and are not changed.

Captured deferred items are encapsulated directly into the task file as items with task_type 'triage' — they are not surfaced to the user for manual routing and are not auto-promoted into new draft seeds.
The task file is a single cohesive artifact per conversation (cohesion by provenance) holding mixed items; each item carries task_type, domain, and deliverable, and is routed per-item.

Build a general two-mode runners map: task_type maps to {runner, mode} where mode is 'inline-skill' (interactive, runs in the main thread) or 'spawn-agent' (isolated own thread). A task type that needs its own thread is registered as spawn-agent.
Routing has two axes: task_type selects the runner; the pair (deliverable, domain) selects the destination.

Skill topology: to-tasks is modified to emit build items plus captured items; dispatch-tasks is a new thin orchestrator that routes; build-code is run-tasks renamed (the inline code runner running TDD); export-tasks is new (the spawn-agent destination writer that absorbs tasks-to-linear).
Names are verb-style, chosen over the <task_type>-tasks convention: dispatch-tasks (dispatcher), build-code (code runner), export-tasks (destination writer).

Skills do not call skills directly; composition happens through the orchestrating agent via the Skill and Agent tools. dispatch-tasks runs build-code inline (TDD needs HITL interactivity) and spawns export-tasks as an agent (mechanical external writes).

The task JSON is the durable state machine. dispatch-tasks is re-entrant and runs exactly one task_type branch per invocation; runners mark each item done in the JSON as they complete, then dispatch reports remaining types and prompts a re-run. This allows breaking context and running branches in separate context windows.

Multi-type ordering: dispatch-tasks announces the full plan up front, auto-orders by a deterministic default, and lets the user override at the announcement (not a forced per-run prompt, not silent). blocked_by dependencies always override the default order.

Default execution order is mechanical-and-cheap first, long-and-interactive last (export-tasks before build-code) so captured deferred items are parked first and can never be stranded behind an unfinished code build. This is a tunable default, not a law.

Destination is classified by deliverable, not topic: an item whose output is a PR/code change goes to GitHub Issues in its repo; any other deliverable goes to Notion (or Linear for corporate/SS). The same topic can split across destinations.

Three destination zones: corporate Quaestor work -> Linear (hard wall); Spawned Sapien -> its own Linear workspace (the SS account's Linear); everything else -> personal Notion DB. Code deliverables in any non-corporate domain -> that repo's GitHub Issues.
Hard segregation walls are corporate and personal; other boundaries are soft. A domain earns its own dedicated destination exactly when another human or a contract enters the picture.
Architectural principle: structure-at-write only earns its place where a mistake is unrecoverable; everywhere else, dump raw and let AI segregate/filter at read time.

Per-item domain assignment: each repo declares a home domain (default by repo); the model reclassifies an item to another domain only on a clear signal (personal is the floater); the task file is the human review gate before any external write; hard-wall crossings (into corporate or personal) are flagged visually for eyeball review with no per-item prompt.

Spawned Sapien gets its own destination because it lives in a separate Linear workspace, a real account wall, rather than living in a shared pool. (This supersedes the earlier rationale that cited an SS Notion DB whose schema matched the personal task list; SS routes to Linear, and the conclusion — SS earns its own destination — only gets stronger under a separate-workspace boundary.)
The personal Notion zone needs an area/project sub-tag; the round-trip pull is always project/area-scoped, never a global personal-priority sort.

tasks-to-linear converts from a skill to an agent and becomes the Linear destination adapter under export-tasks. Linear itself is deferred past v1 (see v1 scope), so this conversion lands when the first Linear workspace is wired.

Config centralizes. The two global routing maps live in one manifest, resources/task-routing.json, with top-level keys 'runners' and 'destinations' — modeled on resources/model-tiers.json (one keyed manifest holding multiple maps, read together by the same consumer). Not split into separate files.

The per-repo home domain is a new 'domain' field on existing entries in resources/repo-policy.json (keyed by Org/Repo). dispatch-tasks reads it via the existing gx_load_policy mechanism in .scripts/gx-lib.sh (git remote -> Org/Repo normalization), reusing the cwd-binding path that already sets GX_LABEL/GX_BASE_BRANCH. No new per-repo file and no new read code.

Vocabulary is plain and reuses existing repo words: 'runners' replaces 'executor registry' (matches lint-runner/test-runner/e2e-runner/runner-result-schema); 'destinations' replaces 'terminus registry'; 'wall' replaces 'segregation_class'. The runners map is task_type -> {runner, mode}; the destinations map is domain -> {adapter, location, wall}.

v1 ships exactly two destination adapters: GitHub Issues (code deliverables, via gh CLI) and personal Notion (non-code deliverables, via Notion MCP). This dogfoods the dotfiles repo end to end and exercises the deliverable-split, since captured deferred items from dotfiles are non-code and land in personal Notion while code items park in GitHub Issues.

Linear is deferred past v1: it is corporate/hard-walled and never receives dotfiles work, and tasks-to-linear is spec-only (no implementation), so converting it is not actually a lower-code path. Future Linear is two workspaces — Spawned Sapien Linear first, personal secondary Linear later — alongside the original corporate Quaestor hard wall.

disposed_threads.text: Should deferred items be surfaced to the user / auto-promoted into new seeds, or encapsulated in the task file?
Use GitHub Issues as the general terminus for all triage items?
Collapse termini to two zones (corporate wall + one consolidated AI-filtered pool) vs three explicit zones?
Multi-type dispatch ordering: forced user prompt vs silent auto vs announce-plan + auto-order + override?
Name for the terminus-writer executor (create-tickets / file-tickets / queue-tasks / create-tasks / save-tasks / push-tasks)?
Where should the executor registry, terminus registry, and per-repo domain binding configs live? Recommended: a manifest in claude-code-shared/ modeled on model-tiers.json, but not confirmed.
Which terminus adapters ship in v1 — GitHub Issues + Notion to dogfood this dotfiles repo, or Linear first via the tasks-to-linear conversion?

deferred.text: Round-trip read side: pull highest-priority items back out of a destination (project/area-scoped) and feed them into /grill-me, closing the ingestion loop.
deferred.rationale: Explicit future phase; v1 covers only the write side (capture + export). The loop closes once the write side is proven.
deferred.context: Destinations are both sink and source. A query like `gh issue list --label triage` sorted by priority, or a Notion query scoped to an area, feeds back into grill-me -> to-seed -> the same pipeline. Round-trip pulls must be project/area-scoped, never global.

deferred.text: Additional runners: investigate, write-pr-desc, and Spawned Sapien business sub-pipelines (video editing, content creation, customer service, sales outreach).
deferred.rationale: Out of v1 scope; each is a future runners-map row + agent. The two-mode runners seam is designed to accept them without redesign.
deferred.context: These are the runners the general map exists to hold. investigate already exists as a stub skill. SS sub-pipelines arrive when the business launches and needs non-dev work streams.

deferred.text: Build out the remaining destination adapters beyond v1: SS Linear adapter (first Linear workspace, from the tasks-to-linear conversion), then corporate Quaestor Linear and a personal secondary Linear, plus the Spawned Sapien work routing.
deferred.rationale: v1 dogfoods GitHub + personal Notion; the remaining adapters follow once the seam is proven. SS routes to Linear (corrected from an earlier Notion assumption), so the first Linear adapter serves SS.
deferred.context: export-tasks resolves (deliverable, domain) to an adapter. v1 adapters: GitHub Issues (code) + personal Notion (non-code). Deferred adapters: Linear (parameterized by workspace) for SS first, then corporate Quaestor and personal secondary.

content.next_action: Run /to-tasks docs/seeds/20260606-0118-dispatch-tasks-pipeline.json to break this into a build plan now that both open threads are resolved.
content.problem_statement: When to-tasks decomposes a seed into a task file, it reads only gate/filter fields (status, open_threads, schema_version, verification, out_of_scope) and never references the seed's deferred[] section. Deferred items — real work consciously set aside for later — are silently dropped. They survive today only because the user personally remembers and re-seeds them, so the backlog effectively lives in his head.
content.solution: Stop dropping deferred items. to-tasks captures them into the task file as triage-typed items alongside build items. A general, re-entrant dispatch layer (dispatch-tasks) routes each item by a two-mode runners map and ships captured items, via export-tasks, to per-domain destinations chosen by deliverable. This turns the lost backlog into durable, queryable triage queues that can later be pulled back into the pipeline.
implementation_decisions: task-schema.json gains fields on each item: task_type (runner selector), domain (destination zone), deliverable (code | non-code, classified by output), and an area/project sub-tag for personal-zone items. Each item carries a status the runners update in place.

The runners map (top-level 'runners' key in resources/task-routing.json) maps task_type -> {runner, mode: inline-skill | spawn-agent}. v1 rows: code -> build-code (inline-skill), triage -> export-tasks (spawn-agent).

The destinations map (top-level 'destinations' key in resources/task-routing.json) maps domain -> {adapter, location, wall: corporate | project | personal}; export-tasks resolves (deliverable, domain) against it and enforces the wall-based hard barriers. v1 adapters: GitHub Issues (code) + personal Notion (non-code).

resources/repo-policy.json gains a 'domain' field per Org/Repo entry; dispatch-tasks reads the per-repo home domain through the existing gx_load_policy path (.scripts/gx-lib.sh), which normalizes git remote -> Org/Repo. No new read code or per-repo file.
dispatch-tasks reads the task file, partitions items by task_type, runs one branch per invocation (build-code inline, export-tasks via the Agent tool), and re-enters until all items are done.
build-code is run-tasks renamed; export-tasks generalizes tasks-to-linear, which becomes the Linear destination adapter (skill -> agent) once Linear is wired — deferred past v1.

out_of_scope.text: Surfacing deferred items to the user for manual routing, or auto-promoting them into new draft seeds.
out_of_scope.rationale: Surfacing scatters work across the user's memory; auto-seeding floods docs/seeds with ungrilled, unverified stubs. Chosen approach is to encapsulate captured items in the task file instead.

out_of_scope.text: GitHub Issues as the general destination for all triage items.
out_of_scope.rationale: GitHub Issues are code-shaped and choke on non-code items (reading lists, research), and a per-repo issue bucket multiplies destinations. Kept only for code deliverables.

out_of_scope.text: A single consolidated flat pool for all non-corporate triage, segregated only by AI at read time (the two-zone model).
out_of_scope.rationale: Corporate is an unrecoverable hard wall (IP/contract), and Spawned Sapien already exists as a separate store; three explicit zones chosen over one pool.

out_of_scope.text: Naming the destination-writer create-tickets/file-tickets/queue-tasks/create-tasks, and naming runners with the <task_type>-tasks convention.
out_of_scope.rationale: 'ticket' is foreign vocabulary in this repo (only used for external systems); 'create-tasks' collides with to-tasks; 'queue-tasks' implies waiting-in-line; the verb convention was preferred for intention clarity. Settled on export-tasks.

out_of_scope.text: Changing the handling of out_of_scope[] and disposed_threads[] in to-tasks.
out_of_scope.rationale: out_of_scope is correctly consumed as a negative filter and disposed_threads is correctly dropped; only deferred[] is the leak being fixed.

out_of_scope.text: Shipping a Linear destination adapter in v1 (whether corporate Quaestor, Spawned Sapien, or personal secondary).
out_of_scope.rationale: Linear never receives dotfiles work, so it cannot be dogfooded in v1; tasks-to-linear is spec-only, so converting it is not a lower-code path. Deferred until the first Linear workspace (SS) is wired.

risks_and_tradeoffs: A solo project's backlog fragments across systems (code in GitHub Issues, non-code in Notion, later SS/corporate in Linear); reconciling priority across them at round-trip time relies on AI-merge and consistent tagging.

Per-item domain classification is probabilistic; a misclassification could breach a hard wall (personal item into corporate, or vice versa). Mitigated by flagging hard-wall crossings in the task file for human review before any external write.
Running one task_type branch per dispatch invocation trades fewer mega-runs for more re-invocations, but buys context-window resilience and resumable state in the JSON.
export-tasks performs outward, semi-irreversible writes (Notion rows, GitHub issues, later Linear tickets); the cross-wall confirm gate is the only guard against an erroneous external publish.
further_notes: Two of the user's own deferred items already graduated into seeds by hand: the gx-toolkit 'PR review-comment to tasks skill' became revise-pr, and pipeline-hardening's 'Judge escalation ladder' became its own seed — evidence the deferred lifecycle is real and currently depends on the user's memory.
The fix for the deferred-drop is itself a previously-deferred item: revise-pr's 'Typed-task / pluggable-executor generalization of to-tasks and run-tasks' specs this exact dispatcher.
Naming intent: dispatch-tasks does no work (routes only); build-code goes left and writes code; export-tasks goes right and ships the rest to its destination.

Config precedent for task-routing.json: resources/model-tiers.json already holds multiple maps (tiers/skills/agents/delegate) under one keyed manifest, read via jq by hooks and via python by scripts. The per-repo binding precedent: resources/repo-policy.json keyed by Org/Repo, read by gx_load_policy in .scripts/gx-lib.sh, currently unused by any skill.

tasks.title: Extend task-schema.json, create task-routing.json, add domain to repo-policy.json
tasks.description: Add four optional fields to task items in task-schema.json: task_type (enum: code|triage, the runner selector), domain (string, the destination zone), deliverable (enum: code|non-code, classifies output), and export_url (string|null, written back by export-tasks after an external write). Create resources/task-routing.json with two top-level keys — runners (task_type -> {runner, mode: inline-skill|spawn-agent}) and destinations (domain -> {adapter, location, wall: corporate|project|personal}) — modeled on resources/model-tiers.json. v1 runners: code -> {runner: build-code, mode: inline-skill}, triage -> {runner: export-tasks, mode: spawn-agent}. v1 destinations: personal -> {adapter: notion, location: personal-notion-db, wall: personal}, spawned-sapien -> {adapter: linear, location: ss-linear, wall: project}, corporate -> {adapter: linear, location: quaestor-linear, wall: corporate}. Add a domain field to each entry in resources/repo-policy.json: Eric-Lingren/dotfiles -> personal, Quaestor-Technologies/Quaestor-Web -> corporate, Eric-Lingren/SpawnedSapien -> spawned-sapien, Eric-Lingren/ericlingren -> personal.
acceptance_criteria: validate-schema.sh passes on a task file whose items carry task_type, domain, deliverable, and export_url fields
validate-schema.sh still passes on a task file whose items omit all four new fields (backward compatibility)
task-routing.json exists at ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/resources/task-routing.json and is valid JSON
jq '.runners.code.mode' task-routing.json returns 'inline-skill'
jq '.runners.triage.mode' task-routing.json returns 'spawn-agent'
jq '.destinations.personal.wall' task-routing.json returns 'personal'
All four entries in repo-policy.json have a domain field

tasks.title: Rename run-tasks skill to build-code
tasks.description: Rename the run-tasks skill directory from skills/run-tasks/ to skills/build-code/ and update its SKILL.md name and any internal self-references. Update every skill that outputs a handoff block referencing /run-tasks (to-tasks, debug, tdd, improve-component, run-task-followups, and any other skill with a 'Next steps: /run-tasks' line) to reference /build-code instead. Update the skill-directory-conventions.md and the skills list in any agent registry or model-tiers-derived file that enumerates skill names. The skill behavior is unchanged — this is a name-only migration.
acceptance_criteria: skills/build-code/ exists and contains the SKILL.md formerly at skills/run-tasks/
skills/run-tasks/ no longer exists
grep -r '/run-tasks' ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/skills/ returns no matches in skill instruction files
grep -r 'build-code' ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/skills/to-tasks/ shows the updated handoff block

tasks.title: to-tasks captures deferred[] items as triage tasks
tasks.description: Modify the to-tasks skill to read the seed's deferred[] array and emit each item as a task appended after the build tasks. Each triage task gets: task_type=triage, domain=home-domain (read from repo-policy.json via gx_load_policy for the current git remote, defaulting to 'personal' if the repo is not found), deliverable=non-code by default (reclassify to 'code' only when the deferred item's text explicitly describes producing a PR or a code change), area_project omitted unless the item text clearly identifies a project or area. Set title from the first sentence of deferred.text. Set description from the full deferred.text plus deferred.context if present. Set type=AFK (triage tasks require no keyboard action — they are routed mechanically). Set blocked_by=[] unless the deferred item's context names a dependency on a code task in the same file. Add a note in the to-tasks skill instructions after the 'Draft vertical slices' section: 'After emitting build tasks, scan deferred[] and emit each as a triage task using the rules above.'
acceptance_criteria: Running /to-tasks on a seed with a non-empty deferred[] produces a task file containing one triage task per deferred item
Each triage task has task_type=triage, domain set to the repo's home domain from repo-policy.json, and deliverable=non-code by default
A deferred item whose text contains 'PR' or 'code change' gets deliverable=code
Running /to-tasks on a seed with an empty deferred[] produces no triage tasks and passes validate-schema.sh

tasks.title: dispatch-tasks orchestrator skill
tasks.description: Create dispatch-tasks as a new inline skill at skills/dispatch-tasks/SKILL.md. It accepts a task file path, loads it, and partitions items by task_type using the runners map in task-routing.json. On each invocation it runs exactly one task_type branch. Before running, announce the full plan: list each task_type branch, the runner it maps to, and the item count; show the default execution order (triage/export-tasks branch first — mechanical-and-cheap; code/build-code branch last — long-and-interactive); let the user override the order at this announcement only (no forced per-branch prompt). Run the selected branch: code branch -> call the build-code skill inline (the Skill tool, inline-skill mode, HITL-interactive); triage branch -> spawn export-tasks as an Agent tool call (spawn-agent mode, isolated). After the branch completes, update each completed item's status to 'done' in the task file JSON by reading and rewriting the file. Report remaining branches with their item counts and prompt the user to re-run /dispatch-tasks <path> for the next branch. If all branches are done, print a summary and exit. blocked_by dependencies always override the default order — a task blocked by a not-yet-done task is skipped in this run and flagged.
acceptance_criteria: dispatch-tasks SKILL.md exists at skills/dispatch-tasks/SKILL.md
Given a task file with both code and triage items, dispatch-tasks announces the plan listing both branches before running
Given only triage items, dispatch-tasks spawns export-tasks as an agent
Given only code items, dispatch-tasks calls build-code inline
After a branch runs, items in that branch have status=done in the task file on disk
After one branch, dispatch-tasks reports remaining branches and prompts re-run

tasks.title: export-tasks v1 skill (GitHub Issues + Notion adapters)
tasks.description: Create export-tasks as a spawn-agent skill at skills/export-tasks/SKILL.md. It reads the task file path passed by dispatch-tasks, filters to triage items with status=not_started, resolves each item's (deliverable, domain) pair against the destinations map in task-routing.json, and writes to the matched adapter. v1 adapters: GitHub Issues via `gh issue create --title '<title>' --body '<description>' --label triage --repo <git-remote-repo>` for code deliverables; personal Notion via the Notion MCP (mcp__claude_ai_Notion__ tools) for non-code deliverables, filing to the Notion DB ID in destinations.personal.location. Before any external write, display a dry-run table showing all planned writes with their destination adapter and URL/DB target; flag any item crossing a hard wall (corporate or spawned-sapien domain item being sent to personal Notion, or vice versa) with a visible WARNING line. Pause for user eyeball review before proceeding. After a successful write, set item.status=done and item.export_url=<external URL or issue URL> in the task file JSON. If the Notion DB ID is a placeholder value ('personal-notion-db'), abort and print instructions to populate FU-001.
acceptance_criteria: export-tasks SKILL.md exists at skills/export-tasks/SKILL.md
A triage item with deliverable=code and domain=personal routes to gh issue create
A triage item with deliverable=non-code and domain=personal routes to Notion MCP
A hard-wall crossing (e.g. corporate item routed to personal Notion) triggers a WARNING line in the dry-run table
After a successful write, the item's status=done and export_url is set in the task file
If destinations.personal.location equals 'personal-notion-db' (placeholder), export-tasks aborts with instructions to run FU-001

tasks.title: Round-trip read side: pull highest-priority items back out of a destination
tasks.description:
Round-trip read side: pull highest-priority items back out of a destination (project/area-scoped) and feed them into /grill-me, closing the ingestion loop.

Destinations are both sink and source. A query like `gh issue list --label triage` sorted by priority, or a Notion query scoped to an area, feeds back into grill-me -> to-seed -> the same pipeline. Round-trip pulls must be project/area-scoped, never global.
acceptance_criteria: Item has been exported to its destination by export-tasks

tasks.title: Additional runners: investigate, write-pr-desc, and Spawned Sapien business sub-pipelines
tasks.description:
Additional runners: investigate, write-pr-desc, and Spawned Sapien business sub-pipelines (video editing, content creation, customer service, sales outreach).

These are the runners the general map exists to hold. investigate already exists as a stub skill. SS sub-pipelines arrive when the business launches and needs non-dev work streams.
acceptance_criteria: Item has been exported to its destination by export-tasks

tasks.title: Build out the remaining destination adapters beyond v1
tasks.description:
Build out the remaining destination adapters beyond v1: SS Linear adapter (first Linear workspace, from the tasks-to-linear conversion), then corporate Quaestor Linear and a personal secondary Linear, plus the Spawned Sapien work routing.

export-tasks resolves (deliverable, domain) to an adapter. v1 adapters: GitHub Issues (code) + personal Notion (non-code). Deferred adapters: Linear (parameterized by workspace) for SS first, then corporate Quaestor and personal secondary.
acceptance_criteria: Item has been exported to its destination by export-tasks
